title: Access Control Event Audit

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Privacy Rule §164.502(a) Uses and disclosures of PHI — physical access-control audit supports the minimum-necessary standard for PHI-storage facilities.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Privacy §164.502(a) (Uses and disclosures of PHI — general rules) is enforced — Splunk UC-15.3.37: Access Control Event Audit.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-15.3.37' running on Access control system logs (badge events, door status), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: Ingest physical access control system logs (badge events, door open/close or forced/held alarms when available) via **syslog** to a Heavy Forwarder, or poll an HTTPS **`/events`** or **`/access/logs`** style export using **REST API Modular Input** (**Splunkbase app ID 1747**) with staggered cron (for example every five minutes) and credential vaulting. Normalize telemetry onto **`index=physical`** as **`sourcetype="access_control"`** with Splunk-extracted fields **`badge_holder`**, **`door`**, **`action`** (for example granted, denied, tailgate), and reader-reported timestamps so `_time` matches the access control engine, not just forwarder receipt delay.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| stats count by badge_holder, door, action
| sort -count
description: We surface data center physical access control activity—badge holder identity, door or reader identifiers, granted versus denied outcomes, and precise event times—so operators can reconstruct foot traffic through cages, meet-me rooms, and high-security suites during investigations.
value: Facilities and security teams keep a tamper-evident story of who was physically present around critical infrastructure, which supports escorted visitor rules, after-hours approvals, union of duty with logical sign-ins, and audit questions about sensitive colocation or equipment areas.
implementation: Route head-end syslog or JSON access events into `index=physical` with consistent `sourcetype="access_control"`. Map `badge_holder`, `door`, `action`, and optional `site` or `reader_id` before enabling CIM Authentication tagging. Schedule the saved search on a rolling window aligned to ingest latency, throttle tailgating duplicates with `stats`/`dedup`, and wire alert actions so physical-security tier-two receives after-hours cage entries while Change Management masks planned door overrides.
visualization: Primary Splunk table sorted by denied attempts with conditional formatting on `action`; paired column chart of grants versus denials by `door`; timeline keyed by `_time` and `badge_holder` for insider-threat reviews; optional floor-plan SVG overlay when `door` maps to coordinates.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration on Authentication.Authentication once props.conf FIELDALIAS/EVAL maps head-end identities (`badge_holder`) into Authentication.user and normalized actions align with Authentication.action.

knownFalsePositives:
• **Planned door maintenance / forced-open drills** — the head-end raises `action=forced` while FM engineers exercise crash bars; suppress events using a CSV lookup keyed by `door` with `maintenance_window=true` synced from ServiceNow change tasks.
• **Expired badge churn after HR termination batch jobs** — Identity Governance pushes revocations faster than local templates refresh; distinguish burst `denied` noise by requiring repeated failures from the same `badge_holder` across multiple readers within ten minutes before paging.
• **Visitor escort badges with time-bound zones** — legitimate `denied` entries appear when guests reach cage thresholds; correlate with visitor management CSV exports (`visitor_id`) and tune alerts to exclude `badge_holder` prefixes tagged `VIS-`.
• **Tailgating replay duplicates** — some controllers emit multiple syslog frames per single swipe; collapse duplicates using `stats latest(_time) by badge_holder, door` before thresholding counts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Confirm the integration described under **Access control syslog, API input** is approved for your facility: credential storage lives in Splunk secret storage or an external vault referenced from `inputs.conf`, not in dashboard tokens.
• Install **REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1747)** on a Heavy Forwarder that can reach the access control HTTPS management plane, or keep a hardened syslog listener on **TCP/UDP 514** with `props.conf` line breaking tuned for your head-end format; follow `docs/implementation-guide.md` for directory layout and upgrade sequencing.
• Create a least-privilege RBAC role such as **`physical_security_readonly`** with read access to `index=physical` and `physical_security_alert_manager` with `schedule_search` + `edit_alert_actions` limited to the UC saved searches.
• Document the vendor export path (for example `GET /api/v1/access-events`) and **TLS 1.2+** requirements in the facility runbook so API pollers stay supportable.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• For API mode, define modular input stanzas with an **interval** (commonly `*/5 * * * *`) that pages results using a high-watermark cursor; for syslog, ensure **TIME_PREFIX**/**TIME_FORMAT** in `props.conf` match the head-end timestamp.
• Stamp raw **door**, **badge_holder**, and **action** fields before lookup enrichment so downstream Authentication model mappings stay deterministic.
• If multiple sites feed one indexer, inject **`site`** or **`datacenter`** at ingestion via transforms so dashboards can split colocation halls.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
• Run the bundled SPL across a rolling **`-24h`** or **`-4h`** window during tuning, then tighten to **`-60m`** once noise baselines exist; save as **Report** before promoting to **Alert** with digest mode.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| stats count by badge_holder, door, action
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL
• **index=physical** and **sourcetype="access_control"** anchor the pipeline to the normalized physical security feed described in Data sources.
• `stats ... by badge_holder, door, action` collapses duplicate reader polls while preserving distinguishable denial reasons when `action` carries them.
• `sort -count` surfaces the busiest doors and identities for hotspot reviews before deeper correlation.

Step 3 — Validate
• Compare Splunk row counts against the access control operator console for the same interval and capture CSV exports plus console screenshots for evidence packs.
• Replay anonymized fixtures on a sandbox indexer to verify field spelling (`badge_holder` vs `cardholder`) matches `FIELDALIAS` rules.

Step 4 — Operationalize and troubleshoot
• Publish a Dashboard Studio table for hot doors and denied attempts, pair with a timeline for investigations, and document escalation when syslog backlog exceeds fifteen minutes or modular input jobs show **429** responses from the `/api/` tier.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab indexer, ingest synthetic `access_control` events where `badge_holder=LAB-ENGINEER-09`, `door=DC1-CAGE-WEST`, `action=granted`, and `_time` falls inside the scheduled search window with volume exceeding your alert threshold; confirm the saved search returns the aggregated row and triggers the configured notification.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Replay only heartbeat keep-alive frames without `badge_holder`, or ingest empty-file polls where `door` is null across the entire window; verify the search returns no qualifying aggregated rows and no alert fires.

evidence: Archive the scheduled search job inspector summary, CSV exports of triggering rows (`badge_holder`, `door`, `action`, `_time`), and the matching access control operator-console audit trail screenshot to your restricted audit_evidence index.
exclusions: Exclude vendor sandbox controllers, demolition halls, and staging racks mapped via `site=staging-*`; route manufacturer telemetry labs to a non-production index so commissioning noise never inherits production thresholds.
equipmentModels: Physical access control head-end exporting syslog or HTTPS badge and door telemetry
